context: Tests whether the agent replaces the ignore_errors anti-pattern with block/rescue/always error handling, uses FQCN modules, replaces command module with dedicated modules, and applies correct boolean syntax. Criteria map directly to error handling, FQCN, and module selection instructions.

checklist.name: No ignore_errors present
checklist.description: The output playbook contains NO instances of `ignore_errors: true` or `ignore_errors: yes`

checklist.name: block/rescue structure used
checklist.description: The deployment steps are wrapped in a `block:` / `rescue:` structure that handles failures and performs rollback

checklist.name: always block for logging
checklist.description: An `always:` section writes deployment status to the log file regardless of success or failure

checklist.name: No command for file copy
checklist.description: Copying or backing up files does NOT use `ansible.builtin.command: cp ...` — uses `ansible.builtin.copy` with `remote_src: true` or `ansible.builtin.file` instead

checklist.name: FQCN modules used
checklist.description: All module references use FQCN (no short names like `copy:`, `service:`, `command:`)

checklist.name: All tasks have name field
checklist.description: Every task in the playbook has a `name:` field

checklist.name: Boolean true/false syntax
checklist.description: All boolean values use `true` or `false`, not `yes` or `no`

checklist.name: Rollback restores backup
checklist.description: The `rescue:` block contains a task that restores the backup JAR and starts the service
